name: badness
description: Language server, formatter, and linter for LaTeX

enumDescriptions: Use the binary bundled inside the extension; fall back to downloading from GitHub if none is bundled or `badness.version`/`badness.releaseTag` is set explicitly (default).
Look for `badness` on the system PATH.
badness.executableStrategy.markdownDescription: Strategy used to locate the `badness` executable for the language server.

badness.executablePath.markdownDescription: Path to the `badness` executable. Only used when `badness.executableStrategy` is set to `path`.

badness.version.description: Badness version to install (for example: latest or 0.2.0).

badness.releaseTag.description: Advanced override for exact GitHub release tag (for example: v0.2.0). If explicitly set, this takes precedence over badness.version.

badness.githubRepo.description: GitHub repository used for Badness binary downloads (<owner>/<repo>).

badness.serverArgs.description: Additional command-line arguments passed after `badness lsp`.

badness.serverEnv.description: Environment variables merged into the Badness language server process.

badness.extraPath.description: Extra PATH entries prepended for the Badness language server process.

badness.logLevel.markdownDescription: Log level for the Badness language server. Maps to the `RUST_LOG` environment variable. Leave unset to use the server's default. If `badness.serverEnv.RUST_LOG` is also set, it takes precedence.

badness.trace.server.description: Trace communication between VS Code and the Badness language server.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run clean && npm run check-types && npm run bundle:prod
scripts.compile: npm run clean && npm run check-types && npm run bundle
